daily_reality.typical_day: Manages calendars for 3 Partners. Books 20+ meetings per day — client lunches, board meetings, investor calls, team offsites. Juggles preferences: Partner A only meets before 10, Partner B never on Fridays, Partner C needs 30min prep between meetings. Handles last-minute reschedules constantly.
daily_reality.biggest_time_waste: Each Partner has their own Calendly link but she can't manage all 3 from one dashboard. Guests book slots she then has to move. No way to block 'prep time' automatically. Cascading reschedules when a Partner's flight is delayed.
daily_reality.weekly_volume: 100+ scheduling interactions per week across 3 Partners
decision_criteria_for_tool: Manage multiple people's calendars from one account
Set complex availability rules per person

psychology.tech_savviness: high — power user of productivity tools, keyboard shortcuts, efficiency hacks
psychology.risk_tolerance: low — can't afford scheduling mistakes with C-suite and clients
psychology.decision_speed: fast evaluator — will test immediately but needs it to work for all 3 Partners
frustration_triggers: can't set different rules per person
no way to approve bookings before they're confirmed
delight_triggers: priority levels for different meeting types

first_screen: Dashboard showing all 3 Partners' upcoming bookings
nice_to_have: VIP guest list (certain clients always get priority)
Meeting type templates (30min call, 60min workshop, lunch)
expectations.current_pain_level: 9/10 — managing 3 separate Calendly accounts is unsustainable
